meta.description: FTO algorithms grouped by subset, then by case. Face letters follow the community notation (Sonja Black's 'FTO Notation' doc + Ben Streeter's FTO Notes); {X,Y} bracket rotations, X2 ergonomic doubles and w-suffix wides are supported. Each subset declares the HOLD its algs execute from ('cif' or 'eif').
meta.status: TCP (last layer) imported 2026-07-10; 1L3T (one-look last 3 triples) imported 2026-07-13 from zwegner’s page by tools/import-1l3t.mjs.

TCP.name: TCP — Triangle + Corner Permutation (Last Layer)
TCP.notation_note: The sheet's header says its algs are written in EIF notation, but machine verification (case layer-locality, the filled-slot criterion, recognition structure) shows every alg executes from the CIF hold — matching the community notation doc's description of the TCP set ('presents its algorithms in CIF orientation, then begins many algs with a puzzle rotation'). Verified 2026-07-10; see tools/test-engine.mjs.
TCP.description: Solves the last layer's three triples (corner + two triangles each) and the filled slot in one look, after everything else is done. 18 cases in three groups: Even (even triangle permutation), Odd (odd triangle permutation, needs a starting AUF — cases 7-10's listed algs INCLUDE the AUF; cases 11-12 are written post-AUF, so do the AUF first), and 2-Flip (the filled slot's triangle already matches, i.e. two triples are flipped in place).
sources: Sheet: Edd Dibley, 'Triangle + Corner Permutation (TCP) Cases and Solutions' (New_Notation_TCP_Full_Diansheng_DaYan) — https://docs.google.com/document/d/16KaLlxaUujgYbCgs-QNCbJbA1Tgc23VsbgMrOhl8gig
[2] Ben Streeter, '[FTO] Introduction to TCP + My Algorithms' — https://youtu.be/iyDDCHQn-T8
[3] Ben Streeter, '[FTO] My Odd TCP Algorithms!' — https://youtu.be/NLJbzBDkGzI
[4] A.B. 'Tipster Tickster', 'All of my FTO TCP algorithms and fingertricks!' — https://youtu.be/ohwcpjqFWb0
[5] A.B. 'Tipster Tickster', 'FTO Algorithms' spreadsheet, sheet 'Full l3t' — https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/11N8tEu0S8aXDUHAsDVJsrh3Uyr6YGn1Asvse4zfAzus

1L3T.name: 1L3T — One-Look Last 3 Triples
1L3T.notation_note: zwegner's page dialect, all machine-verified: S/H are the sledge (R' L R L') and hedge (R B' R' B) triggers (S'/H' their inverses), [U]/[U'] are pre-AUF marks executed as the plain move, {X,Y} bracket rotations as in the community notation. Two source algs are transcription errors (they do not solve last-layer states) and are excluded: 6b.E.5's only alg (the case is therefore omitted) and 4b.O.4's fourth alg. Case 1.E.1 is the solved state. Some algs are marked as working-slot variants or as ending one AUF short — hover their note tag.
1L3T.description: One-look last 3 triples: all 179 cases of the Bencisco last layer solved in a single look. Cases are sorted by OLP (where the top face’s stickers are), then triangle permutation (E = even, O = odd), then corner permutation. Set 6c is the TCP set. Sourced from zwegner’s page; algs by Aedan Bryant, with the 6c algs largely from Edd Dibley’s document.
